evals.prompt: Set up Espresso for our Kotlin Android app with the standard AndroidX Test stack. We're on coroutines for network calls.
evals.expected_output: Should check qa-context.md or ask. Should specify the right AndroidX Test artifacts (`androidx.test.ext:junit`, `androidx.test.espresso:espresso-core`, `androidx.test:runner`, `androidx.test:rules`) with pinned versions. Should set `testInstrumentationRunner = "androidx.test.runner.AndroidJUnitRunner"`. Should suggest placing tests under `src/androidTest/kotlin/`. For coroutines, recommend a `CoroutinesIdlingResource` pattern or an OkHttp idling resource if HTTP is the main async surface — register with `IdlingRegistry`. Should include a starter test with `ActivityScenarioRule`, `onView(withId(...))`, perform/check, and `closeSoftKeyboard()`. Should NOT invent IdlingResource methods.
assertions: Reads qa-context.md or asks for it
Suggests an IdlingResource pattern for coroutines/HTTP
Provides a starter test using the standard API

evals.prompt: Our Espresso tests pass on the developer machine but fail in CI on the emulator. They fail at random points clicking buttons.
evals.expected_output: Should diagnose the standard set: (1) animations enabled on CI emulator — disable via `adb shell settings put global window_animation_scale 0` (also transition, animator); (2) tests racing with async work — add IdlingResource for the relevant async surface (HTTP, coroutines, RxJava); (3) emulator boot not fully complete — wait for boot complete before running tests; (4) flaky setup state — use the test orchestrator (`androidx.test:orchestrator`) for per-test process isolation; (5) device dimensions / locale differences. Should recommend collecting logcat artifacts and using `--info` / `--debug` on the gradle command. Should not pretend single fix solves it.
assertions: Names animation flag as one cause

evals.prompt: how do i tap an item in a recyclerview at position 5
evals.expected_output: Should treat the casual phrasing as a valid trigger. Should introduce `RecyclerViewActions` from `androidx.test.espresso:espresso-contrib`: `onView(withId(R.id.list)).perform(RecyclerViewActions.actionOnItemAtPosition<MyViewHolder>(5, click()))`. Should mention `scrollTo<...>` for finding by descendant matcher, and `actionOnItem<...>(matcher, action)` for matching by content. Should remind the user to add the espresso-contrib dependency. Should NOT invent RecyclerViewActions method signatures.

evals.prompt: We need to test that tapping 'Open Browser' actually launches the browser with the right URL.
evals.expected_output: Should introduce `Espresso-Intents`: add the `androidx.test.espresso:espresso-intents` dep, use `IntentsRule` (or `IntentsTestRule` if on older versions) to enable interception. Use `Intents.intending(hasAction(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)).respondWith(...)` to stub the intent (so the browser doesn't actually open), and `intended(allOf(hasAction(Intent.ACTION_VIEW), hasData("https://support.example.com")))` to assert it was sent. Should use a synthetic URL. Should warn that without stubbing, the test really would launch the browser and may hang.
assertions: Warns about real launch without stubbing

evals.prompt: We've outgrown plain Espresso — we need cross-app flows (system settings, share sheet). Should we switch to Appium?
evals.expected_output: Should explain that you don't need to leave Espresso — combine with **UiAutomator** for the cross-app / system-UI parts. UiAutomator (`androidx.test.uiautomator:uiautomator`) drives the device shell and lets you interact with notifications, system settings, share sheet, etc. Use Espresso for the in-app parts and UiAutomator for cross-app steps in the same test. Switching to Appium is a much bigger move and only justified if cross-platform (iOS) is the goal. Should defer to qa-context.md (do you need iOS too? if not, keep Espresso+UiAutomator). Cross-references appium for the cross-platform case.
assertions: Introduces UiAutomator as the in-stack solution
Recommends combining Espresso + UiAutomator in one test
Holds off on Appium unless iOS is also needed

evals.prompt: Our app makes network calls via OkHttp + Retrofit and tests pass intermittently. We're not using IdlingResource yet.
evals.expected_output: Should identify the missing IdlingResource as the root cause: Espresso doesn't see in-flight OkHttp work and proceeds, sometimes before responses arrive. Recommend either: (1) `okhttp-idling-resource` (community library) or build a custom `IdlingResource` that wraps OkHttp's dispatcher (`runningCallsCount()` + `queuedCallsCount() == 0`); (2) register in `@Before`, unregister in `@After`; (3) avoid the temptation of sleep. Should mention that for coroutine-based code, similar patterns exist (CountingIdlingResource around the coroutine launch points, or test-time dispatcher swaps). Cross-reference flaky-test-management.
assertions: Identifies missing IdlingResource as root cause
Names a way to wrap OkHttp's dispatcher
